claim: INTERFACE DEFECT (mechanical): the rules-1-c freeze 09b246dcba4640581646dee8d8c0ca7def18102f17179ad4398e514ebdd065f6 pinned analysis/rules_1c.py (sha bf4e6476...), whose input contract is argparse-REQUIRED flags (--run-records/--certificate), while tools/registry/verdict-gen.py step 5 (P2 §3.1) invokes the pinned analysis with the eligible results-log run records as JSONL on STDIN and no argv. The pinned script exits 2 on every automated invocation => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS: the frozen record could never produce ANY verdict through the registered pure-function grader, including the INSTRUMENT-INVALID its own rule 0 mandates.
backing_ref: tools/registry/verdict-gen.py lines 529-548 (stdin_payload subprocess.run, no flags); analysis/rules_1c.py main() argparse (required=True); registry/corrections/rules-1-c/2-prefreeze-correction.json 'defect'
rationale: The defect was latent because no rules-1 lineage record had ever reached verdict-gen (rules-1 campaign VOIDED pre-append; rules-1-b superseded pre-GPU). Named so the same flag-shaped analysis contract is never frozen again: prereg-freeze pin checks verify BYTES, not the invocation contract.
notes: Follow-up hardening candidate (not registered here): a freeze-time smoke invocation of the pinned analysis with 0 stdin rows would have caught this at freeze.

claim: Registration mechanism chosen: PRE-FINAL RESET-REFREEZE (docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md 'Scope note'), lawful because both conjunctive conditions held at correction time — rules-1-c absent from registry/gng0-signoff.json frozen_records AND results-log/rules-1-c.jsonl nonexistent (no final-phase run, no unblind; P-9 cutoff not crossed). The 13,470 merged campaign rows sat in results-incoming, never appended. A posthoc-erratum correction alone (a5-llm seq-1 precedent) was REJECTED because it leaves the unexecutable pin frozen (verdict still unproducible by the registered grader); an ops amendment was REJECTED because /pins/analysis_script is design scope (verdict-gen DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES => ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND).
rationale: The scope note's conjunctive test decides the mechanism deterministically; the a5-llm ruling shows exactly when the reset path closes (final-phase runs exist), and rules-1-c is on the open side of that line.
notes: Outcome-neutral by construction: the verdict on these rows is INSTRUMENT-INVALID under both the defective and the successor pin (ASM-1892), so the correction could not move the verdict, only make it producible.

claim: PARITY PROOF: analysis/rules_1c_stdin.py (sha b4fc99000140e29d678dd1a5aa14b65777809a51354f4404ed04019b7436748c) byte-carries every statistic, constant, gate expression and output serialization from analysis/rules_1c.py; only input acquisition changed (verdict-gen stdin eligible-records contract + pinned row/certificate paths, sha256 re-verified fail-closed ERR_RULES1C_*). Measured: rules_1c.py over the merged 13,470 rows + pinned certificate, and rules_1c_stdin.py over a verdict-gen-shaped stdin of 15 stamped eligible run records covering the same (arm,rung,seed) cells, produced BYTE-IDENTICAL stdout — sha256 a45b68123b912d227ebcfd2e4365c16765966a04bcfe58d2e2150a7700885141 for both. Post-verdict re-verification: reports/auto/rules-1-c/analysis-output.json equals the canonical-JSON form of the rules_1c.py reference output byte-for-byte.
rationale: The successor may not be a reinterpretation: byte-identical stdout on the same rows is the strongest mechanical form of 'same analysis, different plumbing'.
notes: Successor also filters pinned rows to cells witnessed by eligible stdin records, so the analysis stays a pure function of the eligible set (an excluded log row drops its cells).
